# Error-Handling Requirements Quality Checklist: Atomicity-test router (read-only classifier) (PRSG-007)

**Purpose**: Validate that the error-handling, exit-status, and never-block requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation
**Created**: 2026-06-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command. It is a set of "unit tests for the requirements" — every item interrogates the WRITING of the requirements (completeness / clarity / consistency / measurability / coverage), not the behavior of any code.

**Focus areas (from request)**:

1. Exit 0 on every successful classification; exit 2 only on usage / unreadable input.
2. Missing or empty `tasks.md` routes to `out-of-scope` (not an error).
3. The three advisory-hint probes degrade gracefully (a probe that can't run emits no hint, never a failure).
4. Never blocking the workflow under any input.

## Exit-Status Contract Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Is the success exit status explicitly specified for every completed classification, including the `out-of-scope` route? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions/Exit-status contract]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the non-success (usage / unreadable-input) exit status explicitly specified and distinguished from the success status? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases/Unreadable or missing input]
- [ ] CHK003 Is it explicitly stated that NO blocking / threshold-exceeded exit status is ever used (i.e. the "blocked" outcome the sibling gate has is deliberately absent)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK004 Are the exact numeric exit codes (0 for success, 2 for usage/unreadable, and the absence of 1) defined somewhere authoritative for implementers rather than left to inference? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions/Exit-status contract]
- [ ] CHK005 Is the error-path output shape (a top-level `{"error": <string>}` object with no `route` key) specified alongside its exit status so success and error outputs are unambiguous? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011a]

## Exit-Status Contract Clarity & Measurability

- [ ] CHK006 Can "successful classification" be objectively distinguished from a "usage / unreadable-input" condition by an implementer, with no overlapping cases? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the term "unreadable input" defined precisely enough (e.g. file present-but-unreadable vs. file absent) that each input file's failure mode maps to exactly one outcome? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases/Unreadable or missing input]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the never-block invariant stated as an objectively checkable property (a successful run always reports success; an error is reported only as a usage/input condition, never as a block)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]

## Missing / Empty `tasks.md` Routing Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Is the missing-or-empty `tasks.md` outcome (`out-of-scope`, a successful classification — NOT an error) specified consistently in every place the spec describes input-shape handling? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, §Edge Cases/Conflicting signals] — RESOLVED: "Unreadable or missing input" edge case reworded to explicitly carve out missing/empty `tasks.md` → `out-of-scope` (success exit), now consistent with FR-003 and the precedence edge case.
- [x] CHK010 Does the "Unreadable or missing input" edge case CONFLICT with FR-003 + the "Conflicting signals / precedence" edge case for the specific case of a MISSING `tasks.md` — one routing it to a non-success error exit, the other to `out-of-scope` with a success exit? [Conflict, Spec §Edge Cases/Unreadable or missing input vs §FR-003] — RESOLVED: conflict removed; the edge case now scopes its exit-2 error path to (a) an absent/unreadable feature directory and (b) any *present-but-unreadable* input file, and states missing/empty `tasks.md` is NOT an error (FR-003 wins). A merely-absent `plan.md`/`spec.md` is tolerated (graceful degradation), preserving never-block.
- [ ] CHK011 Is it unambiguous that an EMPTY (present-but-zero-signal) `tasks.md` yields `out-of-scope` with a success exit and is never treated as an unreadable-input error? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK012 Is the short-circuit ordering explicit that the input-shape check (missing/empty `tasks.md` → `out-of-scope`) runs BEFORE any detector — including the hard-atomic override — so an empty feature dir can never trip an error or a spurious route? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, §Edge Cases/Conflicting signals] — RESOLVED (already satisfied by FR-003 + precedence edge case; the reworded "Unreadable or missing input" edge case now reinforces it rather than contradicting it).
- [x] CHK013 Are the distinct input-failure roles clearly differentiated in the requirements — `tasks.md` missing/empty → `out-of-scope` (success); any *present-but-unreadable* file (`tasks.md`/`plan.md`/`spec.md`) or an absent/unreadable feature directory → usage/input error (exit 2); a merely-*absent* `plan.md`/`spec.md` → tolerated (graceful detector degradation, no error)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, §FR-003, §Edge Cases/Unreadable or missing input] — RESOLVED: edge case softened so only present-but-unreadable files (or an absent/unreadable directory) error; a merely-absent optional `plan.md`/`spec.md` degrades gracefully. This matches FR-003 (only `tasks.md` short-circuits) and the plan's tasks.md-only fixture model, and preserves the never-block invariant when `tasks.md` is present but plan/spec are absent.

## Advisory-Probe Graceful-Degradation Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are requirements defined for a probe whose signal is present but shallow (it is surfaced as an advisory hint only and never forces a split)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, §Edge Cases/Contextual probe signal present but shallow]
- [x] CHK015 Are requirements defined for an advisory probe that CANNOT RUN or errors internally — specifically that it emits no hint and never produces a failure or non-success exit? [Coverage, Gap] — RESOLVED: added a normative clause to FR-010 ("Each advisory probe MUST degrade gracefully: a probe that cannot run or fails internally emits no hint and MUST NOT cause a failure, a non-success exit, or a block") plus a new "Advisory probe cannot run / errors internally" Edge Case.
- [ ] CHK016 Is it specified that the absence of any/all advisory hints is a normal successful outcome (an empty `hints[]` is valid) and never an error condition? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011a, data-model Entity 1]
- [ ] CHK017 Is it consistent across the requirements that the three advisory probes can NEVER, on their own, change the success/error outcome or block the workflow (they affect only `hints[]`)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, §FR-011b]

## Never-Block Invariant Coverage

- [ ] CHK018 Is the never-block guarantee stated to hold "under any input" — i.e. is there a requirement that NO input (valid, ambiguous, empty, unreadable, or signal-free) can cause the classifier to block the workflow? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK019 Are requirements defined for the no-discernible-signal case (abstain to `one-navigable-PR`, success exit, never block, never auto-split)? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases/No discernible signal, §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK020 Is the read-only guarantee (emits exactly one result to stdout, writes no files of its own) stated such that a failure to write can never arise as an error or block? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK021 Is the distinction between an "error condition" (usage/unreadable input → exit 2) and a "block" (threshold-exceeded → forbidden) stated unambiguously enough that the two can never be conflated by an implementer or a downstream consumer? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012]

## Error-Output Contract Consistency

- [ ] CHK022 Is the error-path output (`{"error": <string>}`, no `route` key, exit 2) specified consistently between the Functional Requirements, the Key Entities, and the data model so all three agree? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011a, §Key Entities/Routing decision]
- [ ] CHK023 Is the error message content requirement defined well enough to be actionable (it must identify the usage/unreadable-input cause) without over-specifying exact wording? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011a]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK024 Is the assumption that the exit-status contract mirrors the existing reviewability-gate convention MINUS its blocking outcome stated and validated as the source of the 0/2-and-never-1 mapping? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions/Exit-status contract]
- [ ] CHK025 Is the assumption documented that recording the route (and therefore any record-time failure) is the autopilot SKILL's responsibility, so the classifier's error surface is bounded to read/usage errors only? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-013]

## Notes

- An **unchecked** `- [ ]` item is a SATISFIED (passing) requirements-quality test — the spec already meets it; the item documents the coverage. A **checked** `- [x]` item is one that surfaced a defect (`[Gap]`/`[Conflict]`) that has since been REMEDIATED in the spec (resolution noted inline). No unchecked item is an open issue.
- Items marked `[Gap]` / `[Conflict]` indicated a requirements-writing issue; all such items are now resolved (checked) with the fix annotated inline.
- This checklist tests the QUALITY of the requirements, not the behavior of the script.
- Decided context (do not reopen): exit 0 on success / exit 2 on usage-or-unreadable; missing/empty `tasks.md` → `out-of-scope` (exit 0, NOT an error); advisory hints degrade silently; the classifier never blocks (advisory-only, FR-012).
